name: Nextel Communications, Inc.
description: Nextel Communications, Inc. was a prominent American wireless telecommunications company known for its innovative push-to-talk (PTT) technology, which functioned like a digital two-way radio. Founded in 1987 and headquartered in Reston, Virginia, the company became a major player in the mobile industry before merging with Sprint Corporation in 2005. Following the merger, the Nextel brand continued to operate as a subsidiary of Sprint for several years. The company eventually ceased to exist as an independent entity after its assets were integrated into the broader Sprint network, which was later acquired by T-Mobile US.
